# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_deploy_remote_cluster_askpass_block_is_deduplicated__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/sky/utils/kubernetes/deploy_remote_cluster.sh', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'ASKPASS_BLOCK=' in source, "Expected a factored-out ASKPASS_BLOCK variable to exist to avoid repeating the sudo askpass setup snippet, but no 'ASKPASS_BLOCK=' definition was found."

def test_deploy_remote_cluster_askpass_block_is_deduplicated__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/sky/utils/kubernetes/deploy_remote_cluster.sh', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    inline_marker = '# Create temporary askpass script'
    occurrences = [m.start() for m in re.finditer(re.escape(inline_marker), source)]
    assert len(occurrences) <= 2, f"Expected the askpass setup text to be centralized (at most in the ASKPASS_BLOCK definition plus possibly one explanatory comment), but found {len(occurrences)} occurrences of '{inline_marker}', suggesting it is still inlined repeatedly."

def test_deploy_remote_cluster_askpass_block_is_deduplicated__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/sky/utils/kubernetes/deploy_remote_cluster.sh', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    inline_marker = '# Create temporary askpass script'
    occurrences = [m.start() for m in re.finditer(re.escape(inline_marker), source)]
    uses = len(re.findall('^\\s*\\$ASKPASS_BLOCK\\s*$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE))
    assert uses >= 4, f'Expected $ASKPASS_BLOCK to be reused in several run_remote command blocks (cleanup server, cleanup agent, head install, worker install, gpu operator), but found only {uses} standalone uses.'

def test_deploy_remote_cluster_askpass_block_is_deduplicated__a4():
    source = open('/workspace/sky/utils/kubernetes/deploy_remote_cluster.sh', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    inline_marker = '# Create temporary askpass script'
    occurrences = [m.start() for m in re.finditer(re.escape(inline_marker), source)]
    uses = len(re.findall('^\\s*\\$ASKPASS_BLOCK\\s*$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE))
    assert source.count('echo "$PASSWORD"') == 0, 'Expected the old inlined askpass script form `echo "$PASSWORD"` to be removed after factoring into ASKPASS_BLOCK, but it is still present.'

# Refer to https://docs.skypilot.co/en/latest/reservations/existing-machines.html for details on how to use this script.
set -e

# Colors for nicer UX
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No color

# Variables
CLEANUP=false
INSTALL_GPU=false
POSITIONAL_ARGS=()
PASSWORD=""

# Process all arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        --cleanup)
            CLEANUP=true
            shift
            ;;
        --password)
            PASSWORD=$2
            shift
            shift
            ;;
        *)
            POSITIONAL_ARGS+=("$1")
            shift
            ;;
    esac
done

# Restore positional arguments in correct order
set -- "${POSITIONAL_ARGS[@]}"

# Assign positional arguments to variables
IPS_FILE=$1
USER=$2
SSH_KEY=$3
CONTEXT_NAME=${4:-default}
K3S_TOKEN=mytoken  # Any string can be used as the token

# Basic argument checks
if [ -z "$IPS_FILE" ] || [ -z "$USER" ] || [ -z "$SSH_KEY" ]; then
    >&2 echo -e "${RED}Error: Missing required arguments.${NC}"
    >&2 echo "Usage: ./deploy_remote_cluster.sh ips.txt username path/to/ssh/key [context-na...
        --set 'toolkit.env[2].name=CONTAINERD_RUNTIME_CLASS' \
        --set 'toolkit.env[2].value=nvidia' &&
        echo 'Waiting for GPU operator installation...' &&
        while ! kubectl describe nodes --kubeconfig ~/.kube/config | grep -q 'nvidia.com/gpu:'; do
            echo 'Waiting for GPU operator...'
            sleep 5
        done
        echo 'GPU operator installed successfully.'"
    success_message "GPU Operator installed."
else
    echo -e "${YELLOW}No GPUs detected. Skipping GPU Operator installation.${NC}"
fi

# Configure SkyPilot
progress_message "Configuring SkyPilot..."
sky check kubernetes
success_message "SkyPilot configured successfully."

# Display final success message
echo -e "${GREEN}==== 🎉 Kubernetes cluster deployment completed successfully 🎉 ====${NC}"
echo "You can now interact with your Kubernetes cluster through SkyPilot: "
echo "  • List available GPUs: sky show-gpus --cloud kubernetes"
echo "  • Launch a GPU development pod: sky launch -c devbox --cloud kubernetes --gpus A100:1"
echo "  • Connect to pod with SSH: ssh devbox"
echo "  • Connect to pod with VSCode: code --remote ssh-remote+devbox '/'"
'

# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
from click.testing import CliRunner
import sky.cli as sky_cli

def test_local_up_password_help_mentions_passwordless_sudo_requirement__a1():
    """Ensure the --password option's help explains when it is required."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    result = runner.invoke(sky_cli.cli, ['local', 'up', '--help'])
    assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Expected 'sky local up --help' to succeed; got exit_code={result.exit_code}, output:\n{result.output}"

def test_local_up_password_help_mentions_passwordless_sudo_requirement__a2():
    """Ensure the --password option's help explains when it is required."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    result = runner.invoke(sky_cli.cli, ['local', 'up', '--help'])
    expected_parts = ['Password for the ssh-user to execute sudo commands.', 'Required only if passwordless sudo is not setup.']
    for part in expected_parts:
        assert part in result.output, f"The CLI help for 'sky local up --password' must include the passwordless-sudo note. Missing expected text fragment:\n  {part!r}\nFull help output:\n{result.output}"

# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_askpass_block_is_factored_out_and_reused__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/sky/utils/kubernetes/deploy_remote_cluster.sh').read()
    assert 'ASKPASS_BLOCK=' in source, 'Expected deploy_remote_cluster.sh to define a reusable ASKPASS_BLOCK macro/string for the repeated sudo-askpass snippet.'

def test_askpass_block_is_factored_out_and_reused__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/sky/utils/kubernetes/deploy_remote_cluster.sh').read()
    occurrences = source.count('# Create temporary askpass script')
    assert occurrences <= 2, f"Expected the repeated askpass snippet to be factored into ASKPASS_BLOCK and not duplicated at each run_remote site. Found {occurrences} occurrences of '# Create temporary askpass script'."

def test_askpass_block_is_factored_out_and_reused__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/sky/utils/kubernetes/deploy_remote_cluster.sh').read()
    occurrences = source.count('# Create temporary askpass script')
    uses = len(re.findall('^\\s*\\$ASKPASS_BLOCK\\s*$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE))
    assert uses >= 3, f"Expected '$ASKPASS_BLOCK' to be reused at multiple remote execution sites (cleanup/install/gpu steps). Found {uses} standalone uses."

def test_askpass_block_is_factored_out_and_reused__a4():
    source = open('/workspace/sky/utils/kubernetes/deploy_remote_cluster.sh').read()
    occurrences = source.count('# Create temporary askpass script')
    uses = len(re.findall('^\\s*\\$ASKPASS_BLOCK\\s*$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE))
    mktemp_occ = len(re.findall('ASKPASS_SCRIPT=\\\\?\\$\\(\\s*mktemp\\s*\\)', source))
    assert mktemp_occ <= 2, f"Expected the askpass mktemp creation to be defined once via ASKPASS_BLOCK (optionally plus one extra occurrence in comments/formatting), not repeated across the file. Found {mktemp_occ} occurrences matching 'ASKPASS_SCRIPT=$(mktemp)' (including escaped forms)."

K3S_TOKEN=mytoken  # Any string can be used as the token

echo "You can now interact with your Kubernetes cluster through SkyPilot: "
